briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — finesse post-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for post-spawn with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, grass, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Trim the skirt and add a narrow shad profile soft plastic trailer to improve visual attraction and hooking percentages; bass take the bait deeper and hold on longer than with a skirt alone
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, grass, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hackney: 'Fish a 6.8:1 reel with 16-lb fluorocarbon on a medium-heavy spinnerbait rod. The slower gear ratio helps maintain the medium to slow retrieve needed.'

KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal post-spawn fish hunker down at 6-12ft near cover. Males may temporarily leave fry. Focus on the deeper side of transition areas — channel edges, deeper points, and offshore structure near spawning flats.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
